LRU.description: Least Recently Used — standard doubly-linked list + hashmap
LRU.notes: Best balance of performance and simplicity. Recommended for production.

LFU.description: Least Frequently Used — frequency-based eviction with aging
LFU.notes: Higher overhead, worse for sequential access. Better for repeat-heavy workloads.

ARC.description: Adaptive Replacement Cache — self-tuning between recency and frequency
ARC.implementation_crate: custom (dual LRU lists with ghost entries)
ARC.notes: Best hit rate but highest memory overhead. Patent concerns (IBM) may apply.

recommendation.rationale: LRU provides the best trade-off between hit rate (87.3%), lookup latency (45ns avg), and memory overhead (2.1%). While ARC achieves a slightly higher hit rate (89.7%), its 4x memory overhead and patent concerns make it unsuitable. The 12.4% prefetch improvement with LRU is competitive with ARC's 14.2%.
